howItWorks.intro: This focal length crop factor calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Equivalent Focal Length = Actual FL × Crop Factor
Equivalent Aperture (DOF) = Actual Aperture × Crop Factor
Min Shutter Speed = 1 ÷ Equivalent Focal Length
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is crop factor?
faq.answer: Crop factor is the ratio of a full-frame sensor diagonal (43.3mm) to your camera's sensor diagonal. APS-C sensors have a 1.5-1.6× crop factor, making a 50mm lens act like 75mm on full frame.

faq.question: Does crop factor affect aperture?
faq.answer: It doesn't change the actual aperture or exposure, but it does affect depth of field. A 50mm f/1.8 on APS-C gives similar DOF to a 75mm f/2.7 on full frame.

faq.question: What is the reciprocal rule?
faq.answer: For sharp handheld shots, use a shutter speed of at least 1/(equivalent focal length). A 200mm equivalent needs at least 1/200s. Image stabilization lets you go 2-5 stops slower.

faq.question: Is the Focal Length Crop Factor Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard photography formulas trusted by photographers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Focal Length Crop Factor Calculator?
sections.body: The Focal Length Crop Factor Calculator is a free online tool designed for photographers who need quick, accurate calculations in the photography space. By entering your lens focal length, crop factor, lens max aperture, you get instant results including equivalent focal length, equivalent aperture, horizontal field of view. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting equivalent focal length right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In photography, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by photographers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to capture better images with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your lens focal length and need to find the right equivalent focal length. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
